column1: Update the Status column as tasks are completed.

column4: Task Description
Conduct formal board meetings at least once every quarter and maintain written minutes of each meeting.
Hold formal monthly management meetings with documented minutes (Slack thread, Google Doc, or Notion board is acceptable).
Create a OnePager describing the system and its boundaries and communicate it to a prospect.
Obtain and customize policy templates aligned with your processes; publish them in the internal portal.
Systematically document reference/background checks for all new hires, including informal network references.
Create an onboarding checklist covering HR tasks and access provisioning (who granted what access, to whom, and when).
Implement an offboarding checklist ensuring access revocation is timely and fully documented (who revoked which access, from whom, and when).
Implement annual security awareness training for all employees (Presentation + Google Form will be provided).
Provide annual secure coding training for developers (Presentation + Google Form will be provided).
Publish release notes for clients and create a "What's New" page on the platform.
column9: The communication could be via email

column4: Vendor mapping — review the vendor mapping to ensure accuracy: remove irrelevant entries, retain relevant ones, and add any missing vendors.
Set up monitoring tools for the environment, configure alerts, and ensure ongoing management via alert notifications.
Google Workspace — enforce 2FA for all users.
GitHub — enforce 2FA for all users.
Restrict production environment permissions based on a Need-to-Know / least-privilege basis and enforce 2FA.
Enable Dependabot (or equivalent) in the source code repository and begin addressing HIGH and CRITICAL findings.
Select and engage a penetration testing vendor for the annual pentest.
Ensure all employees have FileVault enabled on their MacBook (MDM-enforced).
Enable WAF or VPC security groups to protect network perimeter.
Create and maintain a system architecture diagram.
Enforce a Pull Request process: all commits via PR, mandatory code review, automated tests, and linked ticket (Linear/JIRA). Merges blocked without these.
Implement branch protection policies: require code review, status checks; define emergency bypass options if needed.
Create a public status page to communicate uptime and downtime to customers.
Configure a formal backup policy (frequency, retention, encryption) for all production databases.
Enable Multi-AZ (multi-availability zone) replication for production databases.
Perform a DRP (Disaster Recovery Plan) test on the dev environment.

column4: Encrypt customer data at rest (database-level encryption) and verify/screenshot configuration.
Ensure all customer interactions use SSL/TLS (HTTPS); document and screenshot the SSL configuration.
Ensure all vendors and partners with access to sensitive information sign NDAs / confidentiality agreements.
Establish and document a formal data deletion/disposal process and policy.
